name: Enterprise APT Intrusion Scenario
description: Simulates a sophisticated APT (Advanced Persistent Threat) campaign targeting an enterprise financial institution. The attacker uses spear-phishing, living-off-the-land techniques, and credential theft to achieve persistent access and data exfiltration.
attack_vector: Spear-phishing email with malicious Office macro targeting finance department employees

attacker_profile.motivation: Financial gain and intellectual property theft

techniques.name: Email Addresses
techniques.description: Harvest employee email addresses from LinkedIn and company website

techniques.name: Scan Databases
techniques.description: Query Shodan/Censys for internet-exposed services

techniques.name: Social Media
techniques.description: Profile finance department employees on LinkedIn

techniques.name: Malware Development
techniques.description: Compile custom CobaltStrike beacon with malleable C2 profile

techniques.name: Upload Malware
techniques.description: Stage payload on attacker-controlled CDN infrastructure

techniques.name: Spearphishing Attachment
techniques.description: Craft malicious Excel macro-enabled workbook targeting CFO assistant

techniques.name: Spearphishing Attachment
techniques.description: Send phishing email impersonating Big Four auditing firm

techniques.name: Internal Spearphishing
techniques.description: Abuse compromised mailbox to send internal phishing

techniques.name: PowerShell
techniques.description: Execute macro that spawns PowerShell to download stage-2 payload

techniques.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
techniques.description: Exploit CVE-2023-23397 Outlook 0-click for initial access

techniques.name: Registry Run Keys
techniques.description: Establish persistence via HKCU Run key

techniques.name: Scheduled Task
techniques.description: Create scheduled task running under user context

techniques.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
techniques.description: Download CobaltStrike beacon and supporting tools

techniques.name: Web Protocols
techniques.description: HTTPS C2 beacon to attacker-controlled CDN over port 443

techniques.name: Asymmetric Cryptography
techniques.description: Encrypted C2 channel using RSA+AES

techniques.name: Internal Proxy
techniques.description: Pivot through compromised workstation to reach internal targets

techniques.name: LSASS Memory
techniques.description: Dump LSASS to extract domain admin credentials

techniques.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
techniques.description: Lateral movement to DC using harvested credentials

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2
techniques.description: Exfiltrate financial records and strategic plans

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.description: Deploy ransomware as distraction during exfiltration

defender_goals: Detect intrusion within 1 hour of initial access
Contain incident before lateral movement to DC
